input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discover latest stable SDK)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios, maccatalyst sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps require the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which come with VS.

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.MSIX.Packaging `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Component.Windows11SDK.22621 `
              --quiet --wait"
```

> If VS is already installed, replace `install` with `upgrade` or use the Visual Studio Installer UI to add the **.NET Multi-platform App UI development** workload.

## 4. Install Java JDK (Android requirement)

```powershell
# Discover available OpenJDK versions
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# MAUI/Android requires JDK 17
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Verify and set JAVA_HOME
java -version
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-Command java).Source | Split-Path | Split-Path, "User")
```

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

The MAUI workload auto-installs some Android components, but you may need to configure them explicitly.

```powershell
# Default location after workload install
$androidSdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# If sdkmanager isn't on PATH, find it
$sdkmanager = Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Path $androidSdkRoot -Filter "sdkmanager.bat" |
              Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty FullName

# Discover available platform versions and build-tools
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"      | Select-Object -First 10

# Install required components (adjust API level as needed)
& $sdkmanager "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools" `
              "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator" --verbose

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses   # type 'y' for each prompt

# Set environment variable
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdkRoot, "User")
```

## 6. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdmanager = Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Path $androidSdkRoot -Filter "avdmanager.bat" |
              Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty FullName

# List available device definitions
& $avdmanager list device | Select-String "id:"

# Create AVD (pick a system image you installed above)
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Launch emulator (optional, for verification)
& "$androidSdkRoot\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_API34
```

## 7. Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading MSIX during development
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"
# → Toggle "Developer Mode" ON in the window that opens
```

## 8. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest -o MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (emulator must be running)
dotnet run -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

> Adjust `net8.0-*` to `net9.0-*` if you installed .NET 9.

## 9. Verify Everything

```powershell
# One-shot health check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --list-sdks
java -version
& $sdkmanager --list_installed
```

---

**Key gotchas:**
- **Hyper-V / WHPX** must be enabled for hardware-accelerated Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
- If `dotnet build` fails with Windows SDK errors, ensure the Windows 10/11 SDK component is installed via Visual Studio Installer.
- The target framework moniker (e.g., `net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0`) must match an installed Windows SDK version.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a comprehensive guide from my knowledge.
